completion_promises.criterion_text: Pull consumer config matches API-nats-pipeline-events.md §2.2 exactly
completion_promises.evidence: build_consumer_config() returns nats.js.api.ConsumerConfig(durable_name='forge-consumer', deliver_policy=DeliverPolicy.ALL, ack_policy=AckPolicy.EXPLICIT, ack_wait=3600.0, max_deliver=-1, max_ack_pending=1, filter_subject='pipeline.build-queued.>'). TestConsumerConfig (8 tests) pins each field individually; runtime check: durable=forge-consumer, ack_wait=3600.0, max_ack_pending=1, max_deliver=-1, deliver=ALL, ack=EXPLICIT, filter=pipeline.build-queued.>.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Valid BuildQueuedPayload → pass to state-machine entrypoint with ack_callback bound; ack deferred until terminal
completion_promises.evidence: handle_message() validates the envelope+payload, then awaits deps.dispatch_build(payload, ack_callback). The ack_callback is a closure produced by _build_ack_callback(msg) that calls msg.ack() only when invoked. TestValidPayloadDispatch::test_valid_payload_invokes_dispatch_build asserts dispatch_build is awaited with the parsed BuildQueuedPayload and a callable. test_ack_is_deferred_until_callback_invoked asserts msg.ack is NOT awaited during handle_message, then becomes awaited exactly once after the callback fires.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Malformed payload → ack + build-failed published; never reaches state machine
completion_promises.evidence: Both MessageEnvelope and BuildQueuedPayload validation are wrapped in try/except (ValidationError, ValueError); both branches await msg.ack() and deps.publish_build_failed(BuildFailedPayload(..., failure_reason=REASON_MALFORMED_PAYLOAD, recoverable=False), feature_id) and return before reaching dispatch. TestMalformedPayload exercises three flavours: invalid JSON bytes, valid envelope with missing-fields payload (still surfaces 'FEAT-OOPS' from the partial envelope), and non-utf8 garbage — every case asserts dispatch_build is never awaited.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Path outside allowlist → ack + build-failed; never reaches state machine
completion_promises.evidence: _path_inside_allowlist(candidate, allowlist) returns False whenever Path(candidate).resolve() is not is_relative_to() any allowed.resolve(); when False, handle_message acks and publishes REASON_PATH_OUTSIDE_ALLOWLIST. TestPathAllowlist::test_path_outside_allowlist_is_rejected uses an outside tmp_path/elsewhere/feature.yaml and asserts msg.ack awaited once, publish_build_failed awaited once with failure_reason='path outside allowlist', dispatch_build never called.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unrecognised originating_adapter → ack + build-failed with failure_reason='originator not recognised'
completion_promises.evidence: After payload validation, handle_message reads deps.forge_config.pipeline.approved_originators; if originating_adapter is None or not in the list, it acks and publishes BuildFailedPayload(failure_reason=REASON_ORIGINATOR_NOT_RECOGNISED='originator not recognised'). TestOriginatorAllowlist covers two paths: None originator and a tightened approved list (["terminal"] only) that rejects 'cli-wrapper'. Both assert msg.ack awaited once, dispatch_build never called, failure_reason exactly 'originator not recognised'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Duplicate (feature_id, correlation_id) already-complete build → ack + skip (no new build started)
completion_promises.evidence: After originator + path checks, handle_message awaits deps.is_duplicate_terminal(payload.feature_id, payload.correlation_id); if True it acks and returns without dispatching or publishing. TestDuplicateDetection::test_already_complete_build_is_acked_and_skipped asserts msg.ack awaited once, dispatch_build NOT called, publish_build_failed NOT called. is_duplicate_terminal is the single read against builds(feature_id, correlation_id) and returns True for any terminal status — COMPLETE included.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Duplicate (feature_id, correlation_id) already-terminal-failed build → ack + skip
completion_promises.evidence: Same code path as AC-006: the duplicate check covers ANY terminal status (COMPLETE | FAILED | CANCELLED | SKIPPED) per the API contract §4 reconcile-on-boot rules. TestDuplicateDetection::test_already_failed_build_is_also_acked_and_skipped exercises the FAILED case explicitly, asserting msg.ack awaited once and dispatch_build never called.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Ack is called exactly once per message — asserted by mock Msg.ack call count
completion_promises.evidence: _build_ack_callback() captures a {'acked': False} flag and short-circuits on the second invocation. TestAckCalledExactlyOnce::test_ack_callback_is_idempotent calls the returned callback twice in a row and asserts msg.ack.await_count == 1. TestAckCalledExactlyOnce::test_rejection_acks_once_no_dispatch confirms rejection paths also stay at exactly one ack with no dispatch.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Non-terminal transitions do NOT ack — ack_callback is only invoked on terminal
completion_promises.evidence: handle_message hands the state machine an ack_callback closure but never calls msg.ack() itself on the accepted-build path. TestValidPayloadDispatch::test_ack_is_deferred_until_callback_invoked asserts msg.ack is NOT awaited during the handle_message call (i.e. across the message's lifetime up to dispatch), and only becomes awaited once after the test explicitly calls the callback (simulating a terminal transition).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Allowlist check uses pathlib.Path.resolve() + is_relative_to to reject '..' traversal
completion_promises.evidence: _path_inside_allowlist() resolves both candidate (Path(candidate).resolve()) and each allowed entry (allowed.resolve()) before is_relative_to(). TestPathAllowlist::test_dotdot_traversal_escape_is_rejected constructs allowlist_root/../../tmp_path.name/outside/feature.yaml — a path that lexically starts inside the allowlist but escapes via '..' — and asserts the message is rejected (msg.ack awaited, publish_build_failed awaited with REASON_PATH_OUTSIDE_ALLOWLIST, dispatch_build never called). The companion test_path_inside_allowlist_via_dotdot_is_accepted proves that '..' segments which resolve back inside the allowlist are accepted, so the rejection is targeted at escapes rather than the '..' character per se.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Both files conform to .claude/rules/code-style.md: PEP 8 snake_case functions, PascalCase classes, UPPER_CASE constants, 88-char max line length (verified by awk: zero lines exceed 88 chars), full type hints throughout, f-strings via logger %-style for lazy interpolation. Black is not installed in the worktree environment so an explicit black --check could not be run, but the source is hand-formatted to Black-compatible conventions (trailing commas in multi-line literals, double quotes, blank-line-2 between top-level definitions).
